step_by_step_thinking: The question asks about the most appropriate statistical test for comparing differences in height across **three** socioeconomic groups. Since we are dealing with more than two independent groups and measuring continuous data (height), one-way ANOVA is specifically designed for such comparisons. Student’s t-test (Option A) compares only two groups; paired t-test (Option C) requires matched pairs—irrelevant here; chi-square test (Option B) applies to categorical data—not applicable for continuous measurements like height. Therefore, Option D is correct.
raw_response:
The question asks about the most appropriate statistical test for comparing differences in height across **three** socioeconomic groups. Since we are dealing with more than two independent groups and measuring continuous data (height), one-way ANOVA is specifically designed for such comparisons. Student’s t-test (Option A) compares only two groups; paired t-test (Option C) requires matched pairs—irrelevant here; chi-square test (Option B) applies to categorical data—not applicable for continuous measurements like height. Therefore, Option D is correct.
\boxed{D}
